title: gymnosporia buxifolia var. venenata
intro: Gymnosporia buxifolia var. venenata, also known as the Poisonous Boxwood, is a plant species that belongs to the family Celastraceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of Gymnosporia buxifolia var. venenata are often used to make infusions, decoctions, or extracts, which are used to treat a range of ailments.
